tests.intent: The reviewed session-bound table SQL macro returns exactly one complete movement result with observable stable contact identities and fractions.
Player radius sixteen, height fifty-six, maximum step twenty-four, view offset forty-one, and two-contact limit come from relational configuration.
A head-on sweep stops the player center exactly one radius before a one-sided blocking linedef and reports its analytic contact fraction.
An oblique sweep reaches exact contact and projects only its remaining displacement onto the blocking linedef tangent.
A corner resolves at most two contacts, retains the last valid point, and exposes both blockers in deterministic contact order.
A two-sided unflagged portal with sufficient current vertical opening admits the complete swept player cylinder.
A step exactly at the configured limit is admitted and destination floor, sector, view offset, and absolute eye height are updated together.
A floor rise one unit above the configured step height blocks even when the portal otherwise has sufficient vertical clearance.
Dynamic sector heights close a two-sided door portal and exact collision stops at its linedef rather than using static map heights.
A portal lower than the full player height blocks independently of floor step and horizontal swept-circle clearance.
The Doom blocking bit makes an otherwise open two-sided line solid and is evaluated independently from sidedef count and opening.
Joint translation of player and geometry preserves relative contact, slide displacement, blocker identity, and vertical result.
A long displacement crossing a thin line uses continuous swept contact rather than destination overlap and cannot tunnel through.
Database-owned noclip bypasses line collision while still resolving destination sector floor and eye-height state deterministically.
Exact swept-circle collision includes finite segment endpoint caps rather than testing only the infinite supporting line strip.
The minimum analytic fraction wins before linedef identity, so an earlier high-id blocker beats a later low-id blocker.
Exactly tied contact fractions are resolved by ascending linedef id independent of candidate row or insertion order.
BLOCKMAP or Spatial candidate reduction is conservative and cannot change the exact result compared with the complete line set.
Portal bottom is the greater current floor and portal top the lesser current ceiling from session-scoped sector state.
Bound relational SQL owns candidate selection, exact contact, stable ordering, two fixed contacts, sliding, and destination state without procedural geometry loops.
Production cannot inspect evaluator artifacts, embed scenario coordinates or expected documents, use dynamic SQL, or delegate collision to client code.
Every isolated collision semantic mutation has an explicit independent witness and a stable detecting test id in this manifest.
